name: Charter Act 1833 (Government of India Act 1833)
summary: Transformed the Governor-General of Bengal into Governor-General of India with legislative supremacy over all territories. Ended the Company's commercial activities. Ordered codification of Indian law. Section 87 prohibited discrimination in public employment on grounds of religion, birth-place, or colour — the first non-discrimination clause in imperial legislation.
key_provisions: Governor-General of India created — superseding all other governors
Company's commercial functions abolished; Company becomes purely administrative body
China and tea trade monopoly abolished
Indian Law Commission created to codify all laws
Section 87 — no discrimination in public employment on grounds of birth, religion, colour, or creed
Legislative Council of India established with a law member (first appointee: Macaulay)
